   785	                  return `${gate}: ${pass}`;
   786	                })
   787	                .join(', ')
   788	            : '[no gate results]';
   789	          return [
   790	            `### Iteration ${entry.run} — blocked by [${blockers}]`,
   791	            `- Recovery: ${entry.recoveryStrategy || '[no recovery strategy recorded]'}`,
   792	            `- Gate results: ${gateSummary}`,
   793	            `- Timestamp: ${entry.timestamp || '[no timestamp]'}`,
   794	            '',
   795	          ];
   796	        })
   797	      : ['No blocked-stop events recorded.', '']),
   798	    '<!-- /ANCHOR:blocked-stops -->',
   799	    '<!-- ANCHOR:graph-convergence -->',
   800	    '## 7. GRAPH CONVERGENCE',
   801	    `- graphConvergenceScore: ${Number(registry.graphConvergenceScore || 0).toFixed(2)}`,
   802	    `- graphDecision: ${registry.graphDecision || 'none'}`,
   803	    ...(Array.isArray(registry.graphBlockers) && registry.graphBlockers.length > 0
   804	      ? [`- graphBlockers: ${registry.graphBlockers.map((b) => (typeof b === 'string' ? b : JSON.stringify(b))).join(', ')}`]
   805	      : ['- graphBlockers: none']),
   806	    '',
   807	    '<!-- /ANCHOR:graph-convergence -->',
   808	    '<!-- ANCHOR:trend -->',
   809	    '## 8. TREND',
   810	    `- Last 3 ratios: ${lastThreeRatios}`,
   811	    `- convergenceScore: ${Number(registry.convergenceScore || 0).toFixed(2)}`,
   812	    `- openFindings: ${registry.openFindingsCount}`,
   813	    `- persistentSameSeverity: ${(registry.persistentSameSeverity || []).length}`,
   814	    `- severityChanged: ${(registry.severityChanged || []).length}`,
   815	    `- repeatedFindings (deprecated combined bucket): ${registry.repeatedFindings.length}`,
   816	    '',
   817	    '<!-- /ANCHOR:trend -->',
   818	    '<!-- ANCHOR:corruption-warnings -->',
   819	    '## 9. CORRUPTION WARNINGS',
   820	    ...(Array.isArray(registry.corruptionWarnings) && registry.corruptionWarnings.length > 0
   821	      ? registry.corruptionWarnings.map((w) => `- Line ${w.line}: ${w.error} (raw: ${w.raw})`)
   822	      : ['No corrupt JSONL lines detected.']),
   823	    '',
   824	    '<!-- /ANCHOR:corruption-warnings -->',
   825	    '<!-- ANCHOR:next-focus -->',
   826	    '## 10. NEXT FOCUS',
   827	    nextFocus,
   828	    '',
   829	    '<!-- /ANCHOR:next-focus -->',
   830	    '<!-- ANCHOR:active-risks -->',
   831	    '## 11. ACTIVE RISKS',
   832	    ...(function buildActiveRisks() {
   833	      // REQ-034 (042 closing audit, F015): surface non-P0 release-readiness
   834	      // debt alongside P0s so the dashboard cannot hide P1 debt behind a
   835	      // "None active" summary when activeP0 == 0. Gate debt on the latest
   836	      // `blocked_stop` and `claim_adjudication` events so operators see
   837	      // claim-adjudication and legal-stop gate failures, not just severity.
   838	      const lines = [];
   839	      if (latestIteration?.status === 'error') {
   840	        lines.push('- Latest iteration reported error status.');
   841	      }
   842	      if (severity.P0 > 0) {
   843	        lines.push(`- ${severity.P0} active P0 finding(s) blocking release.`);
   844	      }
   845	      if (severity.P1 > 0) {
   846	        lines.push(`- ${severity.P1} active P1 finding(s) — required before release; not a P0 but still blocks PASS.`);
   847	      }
   848	      const latestClaimAdjudication = [...iterationRecords]
   849	        .reverse()
   850	        .find((r) => r && r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'claim_adjudication');
   851	      if (latestClaimAdjudication && latestClaimAdjudication.passed === false) {
   852	        const missing = Array.isArray(latestClaimAdjudication.missingPackets) && latestClaimAdjudication.missingPackets.length > 0
   853	          ? ` (missing packets: ${latestClaimAdjudication.missingPackets.join(', ')})`
   854	          : '';
   855	        lines.push(`- Claim-adjudication gate last failed at run ${latestClaimAdjudication.run ?? '?'}${missing}. STOP is vetoed until every active P0/P1 has a typed claim-adjudication packet.`);
   856	      }
   857	      const latestBlockedStop = [...iterationRecords]
   858	        .reverse()
   859	        .find((r) => r && r.type === 'event' && r.event === 'blocked_stop');
   860	      if (latestBlockedStop && Array.isArray(latestBlockedStop.blockedBy) && latestBlockedStop.blockedBy.length > 0) {
   861	        lines.push(`- Latest blocked_stop at run ${latestBlockedStop.run ?? '?'}: ${latestBlockedStop.blockedBy.join(', ')}. Recovery: ${latestBlockedStop.recoveryStrategy || 'see dashboard §BLOCKED STOPS'}.`);
   862	      }
   863	      if (severity.P2 > 0 && lines.length === 0) {
   864	        lines.push(`- ${severity.P2} active P2 finding(s) — advisory only; release is not blocked by P2 alone, but the debt is tracked here so it does not disappear.`);
   865	      }
   866	      if (lines.length === 0) {
   867	        lines.push('- None active beyond normal review uncertainty.');
   868	      }
   869	      return lines;
   870	    })(),
   871	    '',
   872	    '<!-- /ANCHOR:active-risks -->',
   873	    '',
   874	  ].join('\n');
   875	}
   876	
   877	/**
   878	 * Reduce JSONL state, iteration files, and strategy into synchronized registry,
   879	 * strategy, and dashboard outputs. Idempotent: repeated calls produce identical results.
   880	 *
   881	 * @param {string} specFolder - Path to the spec folder containing a review/ directory
   882	 * @param {Object} [options] - Reducer options
   883	 * @param {boolean} [options.write=true] - Write outputs to disk when true
   884	 * @returns {Object} Paths and content for registry, strategy, and dashboard
   885	 */
   886	function reduceReviewState(specFolder, options = {}) {
   887	  const write = options.write !== false;
   888	  const lenient = Boolean(options.lenient);
   889	  const createMissingAnchors = Boolean(options.createMissingAnchors);
   890	  const resolvedSpecFolder = path.resolve(specFolder);
   891	  const reviewDir = path.join(resolvedSpecFolder, 'review');
   892	  const configPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-config.json');
   893	  const stateLogPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-state.jsonl');
   894	  const strategyPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-strategy.md');
   895	  const registryPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-findings-registry.json');
   896	  const dashboardPath = path.join(reviewDir, 'deep-review-dashboard.md');
   897	  const iterationDir = path.join(reviewDir, 'iterations');
   898	
   899	  const config = readJson(configPath);
   900	  const { records, corruptionWarnings } = parseJsonlDetailed(readUtf8(stateLogPath));
   901	  const strategyContent = fs.existsSync(strategyPath) ? readUtf8(strategyPath) : '';
   902	  const strategyDimensions = parseStrategyDimensions(strategyContent);
   903	  const iterationFiles = fs.existsSync(iterationDir)
   904	    ? fs.readdirSync(iterationDir)
   905	        .filter((fileName) => /^iteration-\d+\.md$/.test(fileName))
   906	        .sort()
   907	        .map((fileName) => parseIterationFile(path.join(iterationDir, fileName)))
   908	    : [];
   909	
   910	  const registry = buildRegistry(strategyDimensions, iterationFiles, records, config, corruptionWarnings);
   911	  const strategy = updateStrategyContent(strategyContent, registry, iterationFiles, { createMissingAnchors }, records);
   912	  const dashboard = renderDashboard(config, registry, records, iterationFiles);
   913	
   914	  if (write) {
   915	    writeUtf8(registryPath, `${JSON.stringify(registry, null, 2)}\n`);
   916	    if (strategyContent) {
   917	      writeUtf8(strategyPath, strategy.endsWith('\n') ? strategy : `${strategy}\n`);
   918	    }
   919	    writeUtf8(dashboardPath, dashboard);
   920	  }
   921	
   922	  // Part C REQ-015: fail-closed on corruption. Callers can opt out via lenient:true.
   923	  if (corruptionWarnings.length > 0 && !lenient) {
   924	    const preview = corruptionWarnings
   925	      .slice(0, 3)
   926	      .map((w) => `  - line ${w.line}: ${w.error}`)
   927	      .join('\n');
   928	    process.stderr.write(
   929	      `[sk-deep-review] parseJsonl detected ${corruptionWarnings.length} corrupt line(s) in ${stateLogPath}:\n${preview}\n`
   930	      + 'Pass --lenient to the reducer CLI (or lenient:true to reduceReviewState) to ignore corruption.\n',
   931	    );
   932	  }
   933	
   934	  return {
   935	    configPath,
   936	    stateLogPath,
   937	    strategyPath,
   938	    registryPath,
   939	    dashboardPath,
   940	    registry,
   941	    strategy,
   942	    dashboard,
   943	    corruptionWarnings,
   944	    hasCorruption: corruptionWarnings.length > 0,
   945	  };
   946	}
   947	
   948	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   949	// 6. CLI ENTRY POINT
   950	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   951	
   952	if (require.main === module) {
   953	  const args = process.argv.slice(2);
   954	  const lenient = args.includes('--lenient');
   955	  const createMissingAnchors = args.includes('--create-missing-anchors');
   956	  const positional = args.filter((arg) => !arg.startsWith('--'));
   957	  const specFolder = positional[0];
   958	
   959	  if (!specFolder) {
   960	    process.stderr.write(
   961	      'Usage: node .opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs <spec-folder> [--lenient] [--create-missing-anchors]\n',
   962	    );
   963	    process.exit(1);
   964	  }
   965	
   966	  try {
   967	    const result = reduceReviewState(specFolder, { write: true, lenient, createMissingAnchors });
   968	    process.stdout.write(
   969	      `${JSON.stringify(
   970	        {
   971	          registryPath: result.registryPath,
   972	          dashboardPath: result.dashboardPath,
   973	          strategyPath: result.strategyPath,
   974	          openFindingsCount: result.registry.openFindingsCount,
   975	          resolvedFindingsCount: result.registry.resolvedFindingsCount,
   976	          convergenceScore: result.registry.convergenceScore,
   977	          graphConvergenceScore: result.registry.graphConvergenceScore,
   978	          corruptionCount: result.corruptionWarnings.length,
   979	        },
   980	        null,
   981	        2,
   982	      )}\n`,
   983	    );
   984	    // Part C REQ-015: non-zero exit when corruption detected and not lenient.
   985	    if (result.hasCorruption && !lenient) {
   986	      process.exit(2);
   987	    }
   988	  } catch (error) {
   989	    process.stderr.write(`[sk-deep-review] reducer failed: ${error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error)}\n`);
   990	    process.exit(3);
   991	  }
   992	}
   993	
   994	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   995	// 7. EXPORTS
   996	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   997	
   998	module.exports = {
   999	  REQUIRED_DIMENSIONS,
  1000	  SEVERITY_KEYS,
  1001	  SEVERITY_WEIGHTS,
  1002	  buildGraphConvergenceRollup,
  1003	  parseIterationFile,
  1004	  parseJsonl,
  1005	  parseJsonlDetailed,
  1006	  parseFindingLine,
  1007	  replaceAnchorSection,
  1008	  reduceReviewState,
  1009	};

 succeeded in 0ms:
   150	  ) {
   151	    return null;
   152	  }
   153	
   154	  return {
   155	    run: inferRun(record, statePayload, fallbackIndex),
   156	    replayCount: statePayload.replayCount,
   157	    minRequired: statePayload.minRequired,
   158	  };
   159	}
   160	
   161	function inferProfileId(record) {
   162	  if (record.profileId) {
   163	    return record.profileId;
   164	  }
   165	  return 'dynamic';
   166	}
   167	
   168	function inferFamily(record, profileId) {
   169	  if (record.family) return record.family;
   170	  return profileId;
   171	}
   172	
   173	function buildJournalSummary(filePath) {
   174	  const content = readOptionalUtf8(filePath);
   175	  if (content === null) {
   176	    return null;
   177	  }
   178	
   179	  const events = parseJsonl(content).filter((event) => isPlainObject(event));
   180	  const eventTypeCounts = {};
   181	  let lastSessionStart = null;
   182	  let lastSessionEnd = null;
   183	  let stopReason = null;
   184	  let sessionOutcome = null;
   185	
   186	  for (const event of events) {
   187	    const eventType = typeof event.eventType === 'string' ? event.eventType : null;
   188	    const timestamp = typeof event.timestamp === 'string' ? event.timestamp : null;
   189	    const details = isPlainObject(event.details) ? event.details : {};
   190	
   191	    if (eventType) {
   192	      eventTypeCounts[eventType] = (eventTypeCounts[eventType] || 0) + 1;
   193	    }
   194	
   195	    if (eventType === 'session_start' && timestamp) {
   196	      lastSessionStart = timestamp;
   197	    }
   198	
   199	    if ((eventType === 'session_end' || eventType === 'session_ended') && timestamp) {
   200	      lastSessionEnd = timestamp;
   201	      stopReason =
   202	        typeof details.stopReason === 'string' && details.stopReason.trim()
   203	          ? details.stopReason
   204	          : stopReason;
   205	      sessionOutcome =
   206	        typeof details.sessionOutcome === 'string' && details.sessionOutcome.trim()
   207	          ? details.sessionOutcome
   208	          : sessionOutcome;
   209	    }
   210	  }
   211	
   212	  return {
   213	    lastSessionStart,
   214	    lastSessionEnd,
   215	    totalEvents: events.length,
   216	    eventTypeCounts: sortObjectKeys(eventTypeCounts),
   217	    stopReason,
   218	    sessionOutcome,
   219	  };
   220	}
   221	
   222	function buildCandidateLineageSummary(filePath) {
   223	  const data = readOptionalJson(filePath);
   224	  if (!isPlainObject(data) || !Array.isArray(data.nodes)) {
   225	    return null;
   226	  }
   227	
   228	  const nodes = data.nodes.filter((node) => isPlainObject(node) && typeof node.id === 'string');
   229	  const nodeById = new Map(nodes.map((node) => [node.id, node]));
   230	  const memo = new Map();
   231	
   232	  function getDepth(nodeId, trail = new Set()) {
   233	    if (!nodeById.has(nodeId)) {
   234	      return 0;
   235	    }
   236	    if (memo.has(nodeId)) {
   237	      return memo.get(nodeId);
   238	    }
   239	    if (trail.has(nodeId)) {
   240	      return 0;
   241	    }
   242	
   243	    trail.add(nodeId);
   244	    const node = nodeById.get(nodeId);
   245	    const parentId = typeof node.parentId === 'string' ? node.parentId : null;
   246	    const depth = parentId ? getDepth(parentId, trail) + 1 : 0;
   247	    trail.delete(nodeId);
   248	    memo.set(nodeId, depth);
   249	    return depth;
   250	  }
   251	
   252	  let lineageDepth = 0;
   253	  for (const node of nodes) {
   254	    lineageDepth = Math.max(lineageDepth, getDepth(node.id));
   255	  }
   256	
   257	  return {
   258	    lineageDepth,
   259	    totalCandidates: nodes.length,
   260	    currentLeaf: nodes.length > 0 ? nodes[nodes.length - 1].id : null,
   261	  };
   262	}
   263	
   264	function buildMutationCoverageSummary(filePath) {
   265	  const data = readOptionalJson(filePath);
   266	  if (!isPlainObject(data)) {
   267	    return null;
   268	  }
   269	
   270	  const metrics = isPlainObject(data.metrics) ? data.metrics : {};
   271	  return {
   272	    coverageRatio: isFiniteNumber(metrics.coverageRatio) ? metrics.coverageRatio : null,
   273	    uncoveredMutations:
   274	      metrics.uncoveredMutations === undefined ? null : metrics.uncoveredMutations,
   275	  };
   276	}
   277	
   278	function deriveReplayCountFromDimensions(dimensions) {
   279	  if (!isPlainObject(dimensions)) {
   280	    return null;
   281	  }
   282	
   283	  const samples = Object.values(dimensions)
   284	    .map((entry) => (isPlainObject(entry) && isFiniteNumber(entry.samples) ? entry.samples : null))
   285	    .filter((value) => value !== null);
   286	
   287	  if (samples.length === 0) {
   288	    return null;
   289	  }
   290	
   291	  return Math.max(...samples);
   292	}
   293	
   294	function deriveStabilityCoefficientFromDimensions(dimensions) {
   295	  if (!isPlainObject(dimensions)) {
   296	    return null;
   297	  }
   298	
   299	  const coefficients = Object.values(dimensions)
   300	    .map((entry) => (isPlainObject(entry) && isFiniteNumber(entry.coefficient) ? entry.coefficient : null))
   301	    .filter((value) => value !== null);
   302	
   303	  if (coefficients.length === 0) {
   304	    return null;
   305	  }
   306	
   307	  return Math.min(...coefficients);
   308	}
   309	
   310	function collectSampleQualityMatches(value, seen = new Set(), matches = []) {
   311	  if (!value || typeof value !== 'object') {
   312	    return matches;
   313	  }
   314	  if (seen.has(value)) {
   315	    return matches;
   316	  }
   317	  seen.add(value);
   318	
   319	  if (Array.isArray(value)) {
   320	    for (const entry of value) {
   321	      collectSampleQualityMatches(entry, seen, matches);
   322	    }
   323	    return matches;
   324	  }
   325	
   326	  const replayCount = isFiniteNumber(value.replayCount)
   327	    ? value.replayCount
   328	    : deriveReplayCountFromDimensions(value.dimensions);
   329	  const stabilityCoefficient = isFiniteNumber(value.stabilityCoefficient)
   330	    ? value.stabilityCoefficient
   331	    : deriveStabilityCoefficientFromDimensions(value.dimensions);
   332	  const isInsufficientSample = value.state === 'insufficientSample' && replayCount !== null;
   333	
   334	  if (isInsufficientSample || stabilityCoefficient !== null) {
   335	    matches.push({
   336	      replayCount: replayCount === null ? null : replayCount,
   337	      stabilityCoefficient: stabilityCoefficient === null ? null : stabilityCoefficient,
   338	    });
   339	  }
   340	
   341	  for (const entry of Object.values(value)) {
   342	    collectSampleQualityMatches(entry, seen, matches);
   343	  }
   344	
   345	  return matches;
   346	}
   347	
   348	function summarizeSampleQuality(records, registry) {
   349	  let latestSummary = null;
   350	
   351	  for (const record of records) {
   352	    const matches = collectSampleQualityMatches(record);
   353	    if (matches.length > 0) {
   354	      latestSummary = matches[matches.length - 1];
   355	    }
   356	  }
   357	
   358	  return {
   359	    replayCount: latestSummary?.replayCount ?? null,
   360	    stabilityCoefficient: latestSummary?.stabilityCoefficient ?? null,
   361	    insufficientSampleIterations: registry.insufficientSampleIterations.length,
   362	    insufficientDataIterations: registry.insufficientDataIterations.length,
   363	  };
   364	}
   365	
   366	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   367	// 3. PROFILE BUCKET
   368	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   369	
   370	function createProfileBucket(profileId, family) {
   371	  return {
   372	    profileId,
   373	    family,
   374	    latestRecord: null,
   375	    bestPromptRecord: null,
   376	    bestBenchmarkRecord: null,
   377	    acceptedCandidates: [],
   378	    rejectedCandidates: [],
   379	    benchmarkRuns: [],
   380	    infraFailures: [],
   381	    promptRecommendations: [],
   382	    benchmarkRecommendations: [],
   383	    failureModes: {},
   384	    metrics: {
   385	      totalRecords: 0,
   386	      promptRuns: 0,
   387	      benchmarkRuns: 0,
   388	      acceptedCount: 0,
   389	      rejectedCount: 0,
   390	      tieCount: 0,
   391	      keepBaselineCount: 0,
   392	      infraFailureCount: 0,
   393	      benchmarkPassCount: 0,
   394	      benchmarkFailCount: 0,
   395	    },
   396	    dimensionScores: {
   397	      structural: [],
   398	      ruleCoherence: [],
   399	      integration: [],
   400	      outputQuality: [],
   401	      systemFitness: [],
   402	    },
   403	    dimensionTrends: {},
   404	  };
   405	}
   406	
   407	function incrementFailureModes(bucket, record) {
   408	  for (const mode of record.failureModes || []) {
   409	    bucket.failureModes[mode] = (bucket.failureModes[mode] || 0) + 1;
   410	  }
   411	}
   412	
   413	function maybeSetBestPrompt(bucket, record) {
   414	  if (record.type === 'benchmark_run') {
   415	    return;
   416	  }
   417	  const candidateScore = Number(record.score ?? record.totals?.candidate ?? -Infinity);
   418	  const currentScore = Number(bucket.bestPromptRecord?.score ?? bucket.bestPromptRecord?.totals?.candidate ?? -Infinity);
   419	  if (candidateScore > currentScore) {
   420	    bucket.bestPromptRecord = record;
   421	  }
   422	}
   423	
   424	function maybeSetBestBenchmark(bucket, record) {
   425	  if (record.type !== 'benchmark_run') {
   426	    return;
   427	  }
   428	  const currentScore = Number(bucket.bestBenchmarkRecord?.aggregateScore ?? -Infinity);
   429	  if (Number(record.aggregateScore ?? -Infinity) > currentScore) {
   430	    bucket.bestBenchmarkRecord = record;
   431	  }
   432	}
   433	
   434	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   435	// 4. REGISTRY BUILDER
   436	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   437	
   438	function buildRegistry(records) {
   439	  const profiles = {};
   440	  const insufficientDataIterations = [];
   441	  const insufficientSampleIterations = [];
   442	  const globalMetrics = {
   443	    totalRecords: records.length,
   444	    targetProfiles: 0,
   445	    promptRuns: 0,
   446	    benchmarkRuns: 0,
   447	    acceptedCount: 0,
   448	    rejectedCount: 0,
   449	    tieCount: 0,
   450	    keepBaselineCount: 0,
   451	    infraFailureCount: 0,
   452	    benchmarkPassCount: 0,
   453	    benchmarkFailCount: 0,
   454	  };
   455	
   456	  for (const [index, record] of records.entries()) {
   457	    const profileId = inferProfileId(record);
   458	    const family = inferFamily(record, profileId);
   459	    if (!profiles[profileId]) {
   460	      profiles[profileId] = createProfileBucket(profileId, family);
   461	    }
   462	
   463	    const insufficientDataIteration = extractInsufficientDataIteration(record, index);
   464	    if (insufficientDataIteration) {
   465	      insufficientDataIterations.push(insufficientDataIteration);
   466	    }
   467	
   468	    const insufficientSampleIteration = extractInsufficientSampleIteration(record, index);
   469	    if (insufficientSampleIteration) {
   470	      insufficientSampleIterations.push(insufficientSampleIteration);
   471	    }
   472	
   473	    const bucket = profiles[profileId];
   474	    bucket.latestRecord = record;
   475	    bucket.metrics.totalRecords += 1;
   476	    incrementFailureModes(bucket, record);
   477	
   478	    if (record.type === 'benchmark_run') {
   479	      bucket.metrics.benchmarkRuns += 1;
   480	      globalMetrics.benchmarkRuns += 1;
   481	      bucket.benchmarkRuns.push(record);
   482	      bucket.benchmarkRecommendations.push(record.recommendation || 'unknown');
   483	      maybeSetBestBenchmark(bucket, record);
   484	      if (record.recommendation === 'benchmark-pass') {
   485	        bucket.metrics.benchmarkPassCount += 1;
   486	        globalMetrics.benchmarkPassCount += 1;
   487	      } else {
   488	        bucket.metrics.benchmarkFailCount += 1;
   489	        globalMetrics.benchmarkFailCount += 1;
   490	      }
   491	      continue;
   492	    }
   493	
   494	    if (record.type === 'infra_failure') {
   495	      bucket.metrics.infraFailureCount += 1;
   496	      globalMetrics.infraFailureCount += 1;
   497	      bucket.infraFailures.push(record);
   498	      continue;
   499	    }
   500	
   501	    bucket.metrics.promptRuns += 1;
   502	    globalMetrics.promptRuns += 1;
   503	    bucket.promptRecommendations.push(record.recommendation || 'unknown');
   504	    maybeSetBestPrompt(bucket, record);
   505	
   506	    if (record.dimensions) {
   507	      for (const dim of record.dimensions) {
   508	        const key = dim.name;
   509	        if (bucket.dimensionScores[key]) {
   510	          bucket.dimensionScores[key].push(dim.score);
   511	        }
   512	      }
   513	    }
   514	
   515	    if (record.type === 'accepted' || record.recommendation === 'candidate-acceptable' || record.recommendation === 'candidate-better') {
   516	      bucket.metrics.acceptedCount += 1;
   517	      globalMetrics.acceptedCount += 1;
   518	      bucket.acceptedCandidates.push(record);
   519	    } else if (record.type === 'rejected' || record.recommendation === 'candidate-worse' || record.recommendation === 'candidate-rejected' || record.recommendation === 'reject-candidate') {
   520	      bucket.metrics.rejectedCount += 1;
   521	      globalMetrics.rejectedCount += 1;
   522	      bucket.rejectedCandidates.push(record);
   523	    }
   524	
   525	    if (record.recommendation === 'tie') {
   526	      bucket.metrics.tieCount += 1;
   527	      globalMetrics.tieCount += 1;
   528	    } else if (record.recommendation === 'keep-baseline') {
   529	      bucket.metrics.keepBaselineCount += 1;
   530	      globalMetrics.keepBaselineCount += 1;
   531	    }
   532	  }
   533	
   534	  globalMetrics.targetProfiles = Object.keys(profiles).length;
   535	
   536	  return {
   537	    globalMetrics,
   538	    insufficientDataIterations,
   539	    insufficientSampleIterations,
   540	    profiles,
   541	  };
   542	}
   543	
   544	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   545	// 5. STOP STATUS
   546	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   547	
   548	function countTrailingMatches(items, expected) {
   549	  let count = 0;
   550	  for (let index = items.length - 1; index >= 0; index -= 1) {
   551	    if (items[index] !== expected) {
   552	      break;
   553	    }
   554	    count += 1;
   555	  }
   556	  return count;
   557	}
   558	
   559	function detectDriftAmbiguity(mirrorDriftReport) {
   560	  if (!mirrorDriftReport) {
   561	    return false;
   562	  }
   563	  return /manual-review-required|: missing|Undisclosed surfaces:\s*[1-9]/i.test(mirrorDriftReport);
   564	}
   565	
   566	function evaluateStopStatus(registry, config, mirrorDriftReport) {
   567	  const stopRules = config?.stopRules || {};
   568	  const profileStates = {};
   569	  let shouldStop = false;
   570	  const reasons = [];
   571	
   572	  for (const [profileId, bucket] of Object.entries(registry.profiles)) {
   573	    const state = {
   574	      shouldStop: false,
   575	      reasons: [],
   576	      counters: {
   577	        trailingTies: countTrailingMatches(bucket.promptRecommendations, 'tie'),
   578	        infraFailures: bucket.metrics.infraFailureCount,
   579	        weakBenchmarkRuns: bucket.metrics.benchmarkFailCount,
   580	      },
   581	    };
   582	
   583	    if (state.counters.trailingTies >= Number(stopRules.maxConsecutiveTies || Infinity)) {
   584	      state.shouldStop = true;
   585	      state.reasons.push(`trailing ties ${state.counters.trailingTies}/${stopRules.maxConsecutiveTies}`);
   586	    }
   587	
   588	    if (state.counters.infraFailures >= Number(stopRules.maxInfraFailuresPerProfile || Infinity)) {
   589	      state.shouldStop = true;
   590	      state.reasons.push(`infra failures ${state.counters.infraFailures}/${stopRules.maxInfraFailuresPerProfile}`);
   591	    }
   592	
   593	    if (state.counters.weakBenchmarkRuns >= Number(stopRules.maxWeakBenchmarkRunsPerProfile || Infinity)) {
   594	      state.shouldStop = true;
   595	      state.reasons.push(`weak benchmark runs ${state.counters.weakBenchmarkRuns}/${stopRules.maxWeakBenchmarkRunsPerProfile}`);
   596	    }
   597	
   598	    if (state.shouldStop) {
   599	      shouldStop = true;
   600	      reasons.push(`${profileId}: ${state.reasons.join(', ')}`);
   601	    }
   602	
   603	    profileStates[profileId] = state;
   604	  }
   605	
   606	  const driftAmbiguity = Boolean(stopRules.stopOnDriftAmbiguity) && detectDriftAmbiguity(mirrorDriftReport);
   607	  if (driftAmbiguity) {
   608	    shouldStop = true;
   609	    reasons.push('mirror drift ambiguity detected');
   610	  }
   611	
   612	  if (stopRules.stopOnDimensionPlateau) {
   613	    const plateauWindow = Number(stopRules.plateauWindow || 3);
   614	    for (const [profileId, bucket] of Object.entries(registry.profiles)) {
   615	      const dims = bucket.dimensionScores;
   616	      const dimsWithEnoughData = Object.entries(dims).filter(([, scores]) => scores.length >= plateauWindow);
   617	      if (dimsWithEnoughData.length > 0) {
   618	        const plateauDims = dimsWithEnoughData.filter(([, scores]) => {
   619	          const lastN = scores.slice(-plateauWindow);
   620	          return lastN.every((s) => s === lastN[0]);
   621	        });
   622	        if (plateauDims.length === dimsWithEnoughData.length) {
   623	          shouldStop = true;
   624	          reasons.push(`${profileId}: all dimensions plateaued`);
   625	          if (profileStates[profileId]) {
   626	            profileStates[profileId].shouldStop = true;
   627	            profileStates[profileId].reasons.push('all dimensions plateaued');
   628	          }
   629	        }
   630	      }
   631	    }
   632	  }
   633	
   634	  return {
   635	    shouldStop,
   636	    reasons,
   637	    driftAmbiguity,
   638	    profileStates,
   639	  };
   640	}
   641	
   642	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   643	// 6. DIMENSIONAL TRACKING
   644	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   645	
   646	function topFailureModes(failureModes) {
   647	  return Object.entries(failureModes)
   648	    .sort((left, right) => right[1] - left[1] || left[0].localeCompare(right[0]))
   649	    .slice(0, 5);
   650	}
   651	
   652	function computeDimensionTrend(scores) {
   653	  if (scores.length < 2) {
   654	    return '\u2192';
   655	  }
   656	  const latest = scores[scores.length - 1];
   657	  const previous = scores[scores.length - 2];
   658	  if (latest > previous) {
   659	    return '\u2191';
   660	  }
   661	  if (latest < previous) {
   662	    return '\u2193';
   663	  }
   664	  return '\u2192';
   665	}
   666	
   667	function formatDimensionName(key) {
   668	  return key
   669	    .replace(/([A-Z])/g, ' $1')
   670	    .replace(/^./, (ch) => ch.toUpperCase())
   671	    .trim();
   672	}
   673	
   674	function formatDashboardValue(value) {
   675	  if (value === null || value === undefined) {
   676	    return 'n/a';
   677	  }
   678	  if (isFiniteNumber(value)) {
   679	    return Number.isInteger(value) ? String(value) : String(Math.round(value * 10000) / 10000);
   680	  }
   681	  return String(value);
   682	}
   683	
   684	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   685	// 7. DASHBOARD RENDERER
   686	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   687	
   688	function renderDimensionalProgress(bucket) {
   689	  const rows = [];
   690	  for (const [key, scores] of Object.entries(bucket.dimensionScores)) {
   691	    if (scores.length === 0) {
   692	      continue;
   693	    }
   694	    const latest = scores[scores.length - 1];
   695	    const best = Math.max(...scores);
   696	    const trend = computeDimensionTrend(scores);
   697	    bucket.dimensionTrends[key] = trend;
   698	    rows.push(`| ${formatDimensionName(key)} | ${latest} | ${best} | ${trend} |`);
   699	  }
   700	  if (rows.length === 0) {
   701	    return '';
   702	  }
   703	  return `### Dimensional Progress
   704	
   705	| Dimension | Latest | Best | Trend |
   706	| --- | --- | --- | --- |
   707	${rows.join('\n')}
   708	`;
   709	}
   710	
   711	function renderProfileSection(bucket) {
   712	  const failures = topFailureModes(bucket.failureModes);
   713	  const failureSummary =
   714	    failures.length > 0
   715	      ? failures.map(([mode, count]) => `- ${mode}: ${count}`).join('\n')
   716	      : '- none';
   717	  const latest = bucket.latestRecord;
   718	  const bestPrompt = bucket.bestPromptRecord;
   719	  const bestBenchmark = bucket.bestBenchmarkRecord;
   720	
   721	  return `## ${bucket.profileId}
   722	
   723	- Family: ${bucket.family}
   724	- Prompt runs: ${bucket.metrics.promptRuns}
   725	- Benchmark runs: ${bucket.metrics.benchmarkRuns}
   726	- Accepted candidates: ${bucket.metrics.acceptedCount}
   727	- Rejected candidates: ${bucket.metrics.rejectedCount}
   728	- Benchmark passes: ${bucket.metrics.benchmarkPassCount}
   729	- Benchmark fails: ${bucket.metrics.benchmarkFailCount}
   730	- Infra failures: ${bucket.metrics.infraFailureCount}
   731	- Best prompt score: ${bestPrompt ? Number(bestPrompt.score ?? bestPrompt.totals?.candidate ?? 0) : 'n/a'}
   732	- Best benchmark score: ${bestBenchmark ? Number(bestBenchmark.aggregateScore ?? 0) : 'n/a'}
   733	- Latest recommendation: ${latest?.recommendation || 'n/a'}
   734	
   735	### Repeated Failure Modes
   736	
   737	${failureSummary}
   738	
   739	${renderDimensionalProgress(bucket)}`;
   740	}
   741	
   742	function renderSampleQualitySection(sampleQuality) {
   743	  const hasInsufficientData =
   744	    sampleQuality.insufficientSampleIterations > 0 ||
   745	    sampleQuality.insufficientDataIterations > 0;
   746	  const explanation = hasInsufficientData
   747	    ? '\nSome iterations had insufficient data for trade-off / stability analysis. Review the specific iterations before trusting verdicts.\n'
   748	    : '';
   749	
   750	  return `## Sample Quality
   751	
   752	| Field | Value |
   753	| --- | --- |
   754	| replayCount | ${formatDashboardValue(sampleQuality.replayCount)} |
   755	| stabilityCoefficient | ${formatDashboardValue(sampleQuality.stabilityCoefficient)} |
   756	| insufficientSampleIterations | ${sampleQuality.insufficientSampleIterations} |
   757	| insufficientDataIterations | ${sampleQuality.insufficientDataIterations} |
   758	${explanation}`;
   759	}
   760	
   761	function renderDashboard(registry, sampleQuality) {
   762	  const sections = Object.values(registry.profiles)
   763	    .sort((left, right) => left.profileId.localeCompare(right.profileId))
   764	    .map((bucket) => renderProfileSection(bucket))
   765	    .join('\n');
   766	
   767	  let recommendation = 'Continue only when the next run has a clearer signal than the current best-known state.';
   768	  if (registry.stopStatus?.shouldStop) {
   769	    recommendation = `Stop automatically: ${registry.stopStatus.reasons.join('; ')}`;
   770	  } else if (registry.globalMetrics.infraFailureCount > 0) {
   771	    recommendation = 'Stabilize infrastructure before trusting further comparisons.';
   772	  } else if (registry.globalMetrics.benchmarkFailCount > 0) {
   773	    recommendation = 'Fix repeated benchmark failures before broadening scope or promoting any target.';
   774	  }
   775	
   776	  return `# Agent Improvement Dashboard
   777	
   778	## Global Summary
   779	
   780	| Field | Value |
   781	| --- | --- |
   782	| Total records | ${registry.globalMetrics.totalRecords} |
   783	| Target profiles | ${registry.globalMetrics.targetProfiles} |
   784	| Prompt runs | ${registry.globalMetrics.promptRuns} |
   785	| Benchmark runs | ${registry.globalMetrics.benchmarkRuns} |
   786	| Accepted candidates | ${registry.globalMetrics.acceptedCount} |
   787	| Rejected candidates | ${registry.globalMetrics.rejectedCount} |
   788	| Ties | ${registry.globalMetrics.tieCount} |
   789	| Keep-baseline results | ${registry.globalMetrics.keepBaselineCount} |
   790	| Benchmark passes | ${registry.globalMetrics.benchmarkPassCount} |
   791	| Benchmark fails | ${registry.globalMetrics.benchmarkFailCount} |
   792	| Infra failures | ${registry.globalMetrics.infraFailureCount} |
   793	
   794	${renderSampleQualitySection(sampleQuality)}
   795	
   796	## Guardrails
   797	
   798	- All targets evaluated via dynamic mode; promotion requires explicit per-target approval
   799	- Mirror sync stays downstream packaging and is not counted as benchmark truth
   800	
   801	## Stop Status
   802	
   803	- Should stop: ${registry.stopStatus?.shouldStop ? 'yes' : 'no'}
   804	- Drift ambiguity: ${registry.stopStatus?.driftAmbiguity ? 'yes' : 'no'}
   805	- Reasons: ${registry.stopStatus?.reasons?.length ? registry.stopStatus.reasons.join('; ') : 'none'}
   806	
   807	${sections}
   808	
   809	## Recommendation
   810	
   811	${recommendation}
   812	`;
   813	}
   814	
   815	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   816	// 8. MAIN
   817	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   818	
   819	function main() {
   820	  const runtimeRoot = process.argv[2];
   821	  if (!runtimeRoot) {
   822	    process.stderr.write('Usage: node reduce-state.cjs <improvement-runtime-root>\n');
   823	    process.exit(2);
   824	  }
   825	
   826	  const stateLogPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'agent-improvement-state.jsonl');
   827	  const registryPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'experiment-registry.json');
   828	  const dashboardPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'agent-improvement-dashboard.md');
   829	  const configPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'agent-improvement-config.json');
   830	  const mirrorDriftReportPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'mirror-drift-report.md');
   831	  const journalPath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'improvement-journal.jsonl');
   832	  const candidateLineagePath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'candidate-lineage.json');
   833	  const mutationCoveragePath = path.join(runtimeRoot, 'mutation-coverage.json');
   834	
   835	  const records = parseJsonl(readUtf8(stateLogPath));
   836	  const registry = buildRegistry(records);
   837	  registry.journalSummary = buildJournalSummary(journalPath);
   838	  registry.candidateLineage = buildCandidateLineageSummary(candidateLineagePath);
   839	  registry.mutationCoverage = buildMutationCoverageSummary(mutationCoveragePath);
   840	  const config = parseJson(readOptionalUtf8(configPath) || '{}', {});
   841	  const mirrorDriftReport = readOptionalUtf8(mirrorDriftReportPath);
   842	  registry.stopStatus = evaluateStopStatus(registry, config, mirrorDriftReport);
   843	  const sampleQuality = summarizeSampleQuality(records, registry);
   844	  const dashboard = renderDashboard(registry, sampleQuality);
   845	
   846	  writeUtf8(registryPath, `${JSON.stringify(registry, null, 2)}\n`);
   847	  writeUtf8(dashboardPath, dashboard);
   848	}
   849	
   850	main();

     1	// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
     2	// ║ Deep Research State Reducer                                              ║
     3	// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
     4	
     5	'use strict';
     6	
     7	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
     8	// 1. IMPORTS
     9	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    10	
    11	const fs = require('node:fs');
    12	const path = require('node:path');
    13	
    14	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    15	// 2. HELPERS
    16	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    17	
    18	function readUtf8(filePath) {
    19	  return fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
    20	}
    21	
    22	function writeUtf8(filePath, content) {
    23	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });
    24	  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, content, 'utf8');
    25	}
    26	
    27	function readJson(filePath) {
    28	  return JSON.parse(readUtf8(filePath));
    29	}
    30	
    31	function slugify(value) {
    32	  return value
    33	    .toLowerCase()
    34	    .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
    35	    .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    36	    .slice(0, 80) || 'entry';
    37	}
    38	
    39	function normalizeText(value) {
    40	  return value.replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
    41	}
    42	
    43	function escapeRegExp(value) {
    44	  return value.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
    45	}
    46	
    47	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    48	// 3. PARSERS
    49	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    50	
    51	/**
    52	 * Parse JSONL content into an array of records. Backward-compatible array
    53	 * return; corrupt lines are silently dropped. For fail-closed behavior use
    54	 * parseJsonlDetailed().
    55	 *
    56	 * @param {string} jsonlContent - Newline-delimited JSON string
    57	 * @returns {Array<Object>} Parsed records
    58	 */
    59	function parseJsonl(jsonlContent) {
    60	  return parseJsonlDetailed(jsonlContent).records;
    61	}
    62	
    63	/**
    64	 * Parse JSONL content and report malformed lines (fail-closed pathway, phase 008
    65	 * research reducer parity with sk-deep-review Part C).
    66	 *
    67	 * The reducer exit code is non-zero when corruption warnings are present
    68	 * unless `--lenient` is passed to the CLI (or `lenient:true` to
    69	 * reduceResearchState).
    70	 *
    71	 * @param {string} jsonlContent - Newline-delimited JSON string
    72	 * @returns {{records: Array<Object>, corruptionWarnings: Array<{line: number, raw: string, error: string}>}}
    73	 */
    74	function parseJsonlDetailed(jsonlContent) {
    75	  const records = [];
    76	  const corruptionWarnings = [];
    77	  let lineNumber = 0;
    78	
    79	  for (const rawLine of jsonlContent.split('\n')) {
    80	    lineNumber += 1;
    81	    const line = rawLine.trim();
    82	    if (!line) {
    83	      continue;
    84	    }
    85	
    86	    try {
    87	      records.push(JSON.parse(line));
    88	    } catch (error) {
    89	      corruptionWarnings.push({
    90	        line: lineNumber,
    91	        raw: rawLine.length > 200 ? `${rawLine.slice(0, 200)}...` : rawLine,
    92	        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
    93	      });
    94	    }
    95	  }
    96	
    97	  return { records, corruptionWarnings };
    98	}
    99	
   100	function extractSection(markdown, heading) {
   101	  // Drop the `m` flag so `$` anchors to end-of-string, not end-of-line
   102	  const pattern = new RegExp(`(?:^|\\n)##\\s+${escapeRegExp(heading)}\\s*\\n([\\s\\S]*?)(?=\\n##\\s|$)`, 'i');
   103	  const match = markdown.match(pattern);
   104	  return match ? match[1].trim() : '';
   105	}
   106	
   107	function extractListItems(sectionText) {
   108	  return sectionText
   109	    .split('\n')
   110	    .map((line) => line.trim())
   111	    .filter((line) => /^([-*]|\d+\.)\s+/.test(line))
   112	    .map((line) => normalizeText(line.replace(/^([-*]|\d+\.)\s+/, '')))
   113	    .filter(Boolean);
   114	}
   115	
   116	function parseReflectionValue(sectionText, label) {
   117	  const match = sectionText.match(new RegExp(`-\\s+${escapeRegExp(label)}\\s*:\\s*(.+)`, 'i'));
   118	  return match ? normalizeText(match[1]) : null;
   119	}
   120	
   121	/**
   122	 * Parse a single iteration markdown file into a structured record.
   123	 *
   124	 * @param {string} iterationPath - Absolute path to an iteration-NNN.md file
   125	 * @returns {Object} Parsed iteration with focus, findings, sources, and reflection fields
   126	 */
   127	function parseIterationFile(iterationPath) {
   128	  const markdown = readUtf8(iterationPath);
   129	  const runMatch = iterationPath.match(/iteration-(\d+)\.md$/);
   130	  const headingMatch = markdown.match(/^#\s+Iteration\s+\d+:\s+(.+)$/m);
   131	  const focusSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Focus');
   132	  const findingsSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Findings');
   133	  const ruledOutSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Ruled Out');
   134	  const deadEndsSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Dead Ends');
   135	  const sourcesSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Sources Consulted');
   136	  const reflectionSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Reflection');
   137	  const nextFocusSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Recommended Next Focus');
   138	
   139	  return {
   140	    path: iterationPath,
   141	    run: runMatch ? Number(runMatch[1]) : 0,
   142	    focus: normalizeText(focusSection || (headingMatch ? headingMatch[1] : 'Unknown focus')),
   143	    findings: extractListItems(findingsSection),
   144	    ruledOut: extractListItems(ruledOutSection),
   145	    deadEnds: extractListItems(deadEndsSection),
   146	    sources: extractListItems(sourcesSection),
   147	    reflectionWorked: parseReflectionValue(reflectionSection, 'What worked and why'),
   148	    reflectionFailed: parseReflectionValue(reflectionSection, 'What did not work and why'),
   149	    reflectionDifferent: parseReflectionValue(reflectionSection, 'What I would do differently'),
   150	    nextFocus: normalizeText(nextFocusSection),
   151	  };
   152	}
   153	
   154	function parseStrategyQuestions(strategyContent) {
   155	  const section = extractSection(strategyContent, '3. KEY QUESTIONS (remaining)');
   156	  return section
   157	    .split('\n')
   158	    .map((line) => line.trim())
   159	    .filter((line) => /^- \[[ xX]\]\s+/.test(line))
   160	    .map((line) => {
   161	      const checked = /^- \[[xX]\]\s+/.test(line);
   162	      const text = normalizeText(line.replace(/^- \[[ xX]\]\s+/, ''));
   163	      return {
   164	        checked,
   165	        text,
   166	      };
   167	    });
   168	}
   169	
   170	function buildCoverageBySources(iterationFiles, iterationRecords) {
   171	  const counter = {};
   172	  const seen = new Set();
   173	
   174	  for (const source of iterationFiles.flatMap((iteration) => iteration.sources).concat(
   175	    iterationRecords.flatMap((record) => (Array.isArray(record.sourcesQueried) ? record.sourcesQueried : [])),
   176	  )) {
   177	    const normalized = normalizeText(source);
   178	    if (!normalized || seen.has(normalized)) {
   179	      continue;
   180	    }

    21	
    22	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    23	// 3. HELPERS
    24	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    25	
    26	function readUtf8(filePath) {
    27	  return fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8');
    28	}
    29	
    30	function writeUtf8(filePath, content) {
    31	  fs.mkdirSync(path.dirname(filePath), { recursive: true });
    32	  fs.writeFileSync(filePath, content, 'utf8');
    33	}
    34	
    35	function readJson(filePath) {
    36	  return JSON.parse(readUtf8(filePath));
    37	}
    38	
    39	function slugify(value) {
    40	  return value
    41	    .toLowerCase()
    42	    .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')
    43	    .replace(/^-+|-+$/g, '')
    44	    .slice(0, 80) || 'entry';
    45	}
    46	
    47	function normalizeText(value) {
    48	  return String(value || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim();
    49	}
    50	
    51	function escapeRegExp(value) {
    52	  return value.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&');
    53	}
    54	
    55	function zeroSeverityMap() {
    56	  return { P0: 0, P1: 0, P2: 0 };
    57	}
    58	
    59	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    60	// 4. PARSERS
    61	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    62	
    63	/**
    64	 * Parse JSONL content into an array of records, preserving both iteration and
    65	 * event rows. Backward-compatible array return.
    66	 *
    67	 * @param {string} jsonlContent - Newline-delimited JSON string
    68	 * @returns {Array<Object>} Parsed records (corrupt lines silently dropped — use parseJsonlDetailed for fail-closed behavior)
    69	 */
    70	function parseJsonl(jsonlContent) {
    71	  return parseJsonlDetailed(jsonlContent).records;
    72	}
    73	
    74	/**
    75	 * Parse JSONL content and report malformed lines (fail-closed pathway).
    76	 *
    77	 * Returns both the parsed records and a corruption warning list. The reducer
    78	 * exit code is non-zero when warnings are present unless `--lenient` is passed.
    79	 *
    80	 * @param {string} jsonlContent - Newline-delimited JSON string
    81	 * @returns {{records: Array<Object>, corruptionWarnings: Array<{line: number, raw: string, error: string}>}}
    82	 */
    83	function parseJsonlDetailed(jsonlContent) {
    84	  const records = [];
    85	  const corruptionWarnings = [];
    86	  let lineNumber = 0;
    87	
    88	  for (const rawLine of jsonlContent.split('\n')) {
    89	    lineNumber += 1;
    90	    const line = rawLine.trim();
    91	    if (!line) {
    92	      continue;
    93	    }
    94	
    95	    try {
    96	      records.push(JSON.parse(line));
    97	    } catch (error) {
    98	      corruptionWarnings.push({
    99	        line: lineNumber,
   100	        raw: rawLine.length > 200 ? `${rawLine.slice(0, 200)}...` : rawLine,
   101	        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
   102	      });
   103	    }
   104	  }
   105	
   106	  return { records, corruptionWarnings };
   107	}
   108	
   109	function extractSection(markdown, heading) {
   110	  const pattern = new RegExp(`(?:^|\\n)##\\s+${escapeRegExp(heading)}\\s*\\n([\\s\\S]*?)(?=\\n##\\s|$)`, 'i');
   111	  const match = markdown.match(pattern);
   112	  return match ? match[1].trim() : '';
   113	}
   114	
   115	function extractSubsection(sectionText, subheading) {
   116	  const pattern = new RegExp(`(?:^|\\n)###\\s+${escapeRegExp(subheading)}[^\\n]*\\n([\\s\\S]*?)(?=\\n###\\s|\\n##\\s|$)`, 'i');
   117	  const match = sectionText.match(pattern);
   118	  return match ? match[1].trim() : '';
   119	}
   120	
   121	function extractListItems(sectionText) {
   122	  return sectionText
   123	    .split('\n')
   124	    .map((line) => line.trim())
   125	    .filter((line) => /^([-*]|\d+\.)\s+/.test(line))
   126	    .map((line) => normalizeText(line.replace(/^([-*]|\d+\.)\s+/, '')))
   127	    .filter(Boolean);
   128	}
   129	
   130	/**
   131	 * Parse a finding line of the form: `- **F001**: Title — file:line — Description`
   132	 *
   133	 * @param {string} line - Raw finding bullet
   134	 * @param {string} severity - P0, P1, or P2 context
   135	 * @returns {Object|null} Structured finding or null when the line is not a finding
   136	 */
   137	function parseFindingLine(line, severity) {
   138	  const match = line.match(/^\*\*(F\d+)\*\*\s*:\s*(.+?)(?:\s+[—-]\s+`?([^`]+?)`?)?(?:\s+[—-]\s+(.+))?$/);
   139	  if (!match) {
   140	    return null;
   141	  }
   142	
   143	  const [, findingId, title, evidenceRaw, description] = match;
   144	  let file = null;
   145	  let lineNumber = null;
   146	
   147	  if (evidenceRaw) {
   148	    const evidenceMatch = evidenceRaw.trim().match(/^(.+?):(\d+)(?:[:-].*)?$/);
   149	    if (evidenceMatch) {
   150	      file = evidenceMatch[1];
   151	      lineNumber = Number(evidenceMatch[2]);
   152	    } else {
   153	      file = evidenceRaw.trim();
   154	    }
   155	  }
   156	
   157	  return {
   158	    findingId,
   159	    severity,
   160	    title: normalizeText(title),
   161	    file,
   162	    line: lineNumber,
   163	    description: normalizeText(description || ''),
   164	  };
   165	}
   166	
   167	function parseFindingsBlock(sectionText, severity) {
   168	  if (!sectionText) {
   169	    return [];
   170	  }
   171	
   172	  return sectionText
   173	    .split('\n')
   174	    .map((line) => line.trim())
   175	    .filter((line) => /^-\s+\*\*F\d+\*\*/.test(line))
   176	    .map((line) => parseFindingLine(line.replace(/^-\s+/, ''), severity))
   177	    .filter(Boolean);
   178	}
   179	
   180	/**
   181	 * Parse a single iteration markdown file into a structured review record.
   182	 *
   183	 * @param {string} iterationPath - Absolute path to an iteration-NNN.md file
   184	 * @returns {Object} Parsed iteration with focus, findings, cross-reference, and reflection fields
   185	 */
   186	function parseIterationFile(iterationPath) {
   187	  const markdown = readUtf8(iterationPath);
   188	  const runMatch = iterationPath.match(/iteration-(\d+)\.md$/);
   189	  const headingMatch = markdown.match(/^#\s+Iteration\s+\d+:\s+(.+)$/m);
   190	
   191	  const focusSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Focus');
   192	  const findingsSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Findings');
   193	  const ruledOutSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Ruled Out');
   194	  const deadEndsSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Dead Ends');
   195	  const nextFocusSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Recommended Next Focus');
   196	  const assessmentSection = extractSection(markdown, 'Assessment');
   197	
   198	  const p0Block = extractSubsection(findingsSection, 'P0');
   199	  const p1Block = extractSubsection(findingsSection, 'P1');
   200	  const p2Block = extractSubsection(findingsSection, 'P2');
   201	
   202	  const findings = [
   203	    ...parseFindingsBlock(p0Block, 'P0'),
   204	    ...parseFindingsBlock(p1Block, 'P1'),
   205	    ...parseFindingsBlock(p2Block, 'P2'),
   206	  ];
   207	
   208	  const dimensionsAddressed = (assessmentSection.match(/Dimensions addressed:\s*(.+)/i) || [])[1];
   209	
   210	  return {
   211	    path: iterationPath,
   212	    run: runMatch ? Number(runMatch[1]) : 0,
   213	    focus: normalizeText(focusSection || (headingMatch ? headingMatch[1] : 'Unknown focus')),
   214	    findings,
   215	    ruledOut: extractListItems(ruledOutSection),
   216	    deadEnds: extractListItems(deadEndsSection),
   217	    nextFocus: normalizeText(nextFocusSection),
   218	    dimensionsAddressed: dimensionsAddressed
   219	      ? dimensionsAddressed.split(/[,;]/).map((value) => normalizeText(value).toLowerCase()).filter(Boolean)
   220	      : [],
   221	  };
   222	}
   223	
   224	function parseStrategyDimensions(strategyContent) {
   225	  const section = extractSection(strategyContent, '3. REVIEW DIMENSIONS (remaining)')
   226	    || extractSection(strategyContent, '3. KEY QUESTIONS (remaining)');
   227	  return section
   228	    .split('\n')
   229	    .map((line) => line.trim())
   230	    .filter((line) => /^- \[[ xX]\]\s+/.test(line))
   231	    .map((line) => {
   232	      const checked = /^- \[[xX]\]\s+/.test(line);
   233	      const text = normalizeText(line.replace(/^- \[[ xX]\]\s+/, ''));
   234	      return { checked, text };
   235	    });
   236	}
   237	
   238	function uniqueById(items) {
   239	  const seen = new Set();
   240	  const result = [];
   241	
   242	  for (const item of items) {
   243	    if (seen.has(item.findingId)) {
   244	      continue;
   245	    }
   246	    seen.add(item.findingId);
   247	    result.push(item);
   248	  }
   249	
   250	  return result;
   251	}
   252	
   253	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   254	// 5. CORE LOGIC
   255	// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   256	
   257	function buildFindingRegistry(iterationFiles, iterationRecords) {
   258	  const findingById = new Map();
   259	
   260	  for (const iteration of iterationFiles) {
   261	    for (const finding of iteration.findings) {
   262	      const existing = findingById.get(finding.findingId);
   263	      if (!existing) {
   264	        findingById.set(finding.findingId, {
   265	          ...finding,
   266	          dimension: deriveDimension(finding, iteration),
   267	          firstSeen: iteration.run,
   268	          lastSeen: iteration.run,
   269	          status: 'active',
   270	          transitions: [{
   271	            iteration: iteration.run,
   272	            from: null,
   273	            to: finding.severity,
   274	            reason: 'Initial discovery',
   275	          }],
   276	        });
   277	      } else {
   278	        existing.lastSeen = iteration.run;
   279	        if (existing.severity !== finding.severity) {
   280	          existing.transitions.push({
   281	            iteration: iteration.run,
   282	            from: existing.severity,
   283	            to: finding.severity,
   284	            reason: 'Severity adjusted in later iteration',
   285	          });
   286	          existing.severity = finding.severity;
   287	        }
   288	      }
   289	    }
   290	  }
   291	
   292	  const resolvedIdSet = new Set();
   293	  for (const record of iterationRecords) {
   294	    if (Array.isArray(record.resolvedFindings)) {
   295	      for (const id of record.resolvedFindings) {
   296	        resolvedIdSet.add(id);
   297	      }
   298	    }
   299	  }
   300	
   301	  const openFindings = [];
   302	  const resolvedFindings = [];
   303	  for (const finding of findingById.values()) {
   304	    if (resolvedIdSet.has(finding.findingId)) {
   305	      finding.status = 'resolved';
   306	      resolvedFindings.push(finding);
   307	    } else {
   308	      openFindings.push(finding);
   309	    }
   310	  }
   311	
   312	  openFindings.sort(compareFindings);
   313	  resolvedFindings.sort(compareFindings);
   314	
   315	  return { openFindings, resolvedFindings };
   316	}
   317	
   318	function compareFindings(left, right) {
   319	  const severityOrder = { P0: 0, P1: 1, P2: 2 };
   320	  const severityDiff = (severityOrder[left.severity] ?? 9) - (severityOrder[right.severity] ?? 9);

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:3:description: "Completed Level 3 phase packet for the deep-loop runtime-truth alignment that introduced journal wiring, mutation coverage, trade-off detection, candidate lineage, and benchmark stability for the skill now published as sk-improve-agent."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:23:Phase 005 completed the runtime-truth alignment for the improve-agent skill family. The delivered work added an append-only audit journal, an improvement-scoped mutation coverage graph, dimension trade-off detection, candidate lineage tracking for optional parallel waves, and benchmark stability scoring so the improver loop could behave like the deeper research and review loops instead of remaining an isolated evaluator.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:56:Before this phase shipped, the improve-agent loop had strong evaluator logic but weak runtime truth. It lacked a dedicated audit journal, had no improvement-scoped coverage graph, could not detect cross-dimension trade-offs, had no candidate-lineage model for parallel exploration, and could not measure benchmark replay stability. That left the loop harder to audit, less explainable, and less aligned with the runtime-truth contracts already established for `sk-deep-research` and `sk-deep-review`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:62:- append-only journal wiring through `improvement-journal.cjs`

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:386:description: "Transfer the proven deep-loop runtime truth contracts from 042 Phases 1-4 to the sk-improve-agent skill, adding stop-reason taxonomy, audit journaling, mutation coverage tracking, trade-off detection, optional parallel candidates, and scoring weight optimization."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:391:  - "improvement journal"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:407:The sk-improve-agent skill runs bounded improvement loops over agent packets, but it currently lacks the runtime truth infrastructure proven in 042 Phases 1-4 for deep-research and deep-review. This phase ports those contracts into agent-improver: a well-formed stop-reason taxonomy, legal-stop gates, audit journaling, mutation coverage tracking, dimension trajectory analysis, trade-off detection, optional parallel candidate waves, and scoring weight optimization based on session history.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:409:**Key Decisions**: emit journal events in the orchestrator (not in the proposal-only agent); reuse the existing coverage graph infrastructure with an improvement-specific namespace; treat dimension trajectory as a first-class convergence signal; keep parallel candidate waves opt-in behind an exploration-breadth gate.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:411:**Critical Dependencies**: 042 Phase 1 runtime truth foundation (stop-reason taxonomy, event journal schema); 042 Phase 2 semantic coverage graph (reused with `loop_type: "improvement"`); existing sk-improve-agent 5-dimension scoring and packet-local candidate generation discipline.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:441:Apply the same runtime truth contracts to agent-improver so that every improvement session is auditable, resumable, convergence-aware, and self-improving over time.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:452:- Audit journal (`improvement-journal.jsonl`) capturing all session events

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:482:| `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs` | Create | Append-only JSONL event emitter for improvement session events |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:487:| `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/tests/improvement-journal.vitest.ts` | Create | Tests for journal emit, append-only enforcement, event schema |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:505:| REQ-AI-003 | Resume/continuation semantics for improvement sessions | A session started with a prior session-id replays journal state and resumes from the last checkpoint without repeating completed iterations |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:506:| REQ-AI-004 | Audit journal (`improvement-journal.jsonl`) capturing all events (iteration-started, candidate-proposed, candidate-evaluated, promotion-gate-checked, session-ended) | All five event types appear in the journal for every completed session; file is append-only and survives process restart |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:514:| REQ-AI-007 | Dimension trajectory tracking (per-dimension score history across iterations) | For each active session the trajectory file contains a time-ordered score vector per dimension; minimum 3 data points before convergence can be claimed |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:515:| REQ-AI-008 | Trade-off detection flagging when dimension A improves by >threshold while dimension B regresses by >threshold | The detector emits a `trade-off-detected` event to the journal whenever thresholds are crossed; thresholds are configurable |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:529:| REQ-AI-012 | Scoring weight optimizer using historical session data to recommend per-dimension weight adjustments | After a configurable number of sessions the optimizer reads the journal history and emits a weight-recommendation report; recommendations do not auto-apply without explicit approval |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:530:| REQ-AI-013 | Benchmark replay stability measurement confirming consistent scores across identical replays | The benchmark-stability script reports a stability coefficient for each dimension; sessions with coefficient below threshold emit a `stabilityWarning` to the journal |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:538:- **SC-001**: Every improvement session termination produces a journal entry with exactly one stop-reason from the approved taxonomy.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:540:- **SC-003**: A session resumed from a prior session-id replays correctly and does not duplicate iteration events.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:556:| Dependency | 042 Phase 1 journal schema | Journal emit contract must be compatible | Use same event schema from Phase 1; document any improvement-specific extensions |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:558:| Dependency | sk-improve-agent proposal-only constraint | Orchestrator must own journal emission; agent must not write state | ADR-001 enforces this; enforce via code review gate |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:559:| Risk | Trajectory-based convergence false positives | Session terminates too early on noisy score data | Require minimum 3 trajectory data points before convergence claim (REQ-AI-007) |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:595:- All mutation types exhausted before convergence: emit `maxIterationsReached` with exhausted-mutations annotation; do not loop indefinitely.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:608:description: "4-sub-phase plan porting 042 runtime truth contracts to sk-improve-agent: stop-reason taxonomy, audit journal, mutation coverage graph, trade-off detection, optional parallel candidate waves, and scoring weight optimization."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:612:  - "improvement journal implementation"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:634:| **Storage** | JSONL files (journal, ledger), JSON files (coverage graph, lineage, trajectory) |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:639:This plan ports the runtime truth infrastructure proven in 042 Phases 1-4 into the sk-improve-agent skill across four sub-phases. Sub-phase 5a delivers the core runtime contracts (stop-reason taxonomy, legal-stop gates, audit journal, hypothesis ledger). Sub-phase 5b adds improvement intelligence (mutation coverage graph, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, exhausted-mutations log). Sub-phase 5c adds optional parallel candidate waves and candidate lineage. Sub-phase 5d adds scoring weight optimization and benchmark replay stability. All new config fields are optional with defaults, preserving backward compatibility.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:649:- [ ] 042 Phase 1 journal schema confirmed and compatible with improvement events
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:671:Event-sourced improvement loop with packet-local append-only state. The orchestrator owns all state writes; the proposal-only agent remains read-only relative to journals and graphs.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:675:- **improvement-journal.cjs**: Append-only JSONL emitter. Receives structured event objects from the orchestrator and appends them to the session journal file. Enforces the event schema and blocks invalid event types.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:677:- **trade-off-detector.cjs**: Reads the current dimension trajectory and compares per-dimension deltas against configured thresholds. Emits a structured trade-off report when thresholds are crossed; used by the orchestrator to decide whether to journal a `trade-off-detected` event.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:683:Orchestrator receives improvement session trigger → reads prior journal (if resume) → enters iteration loop → calls agent-improver (proposal-only) → evaluates candidate using 5-dimension scoring → calls trade-off-detector → appends events to journal via improvement-journal.cjs → updates mutation-coverage.cjs → checks legal-stop gates → emits stop-reason and session-ended event → optionally triggers benchmark-stability.cjs post-session → optionally triggers weight optimizer (separate invocation).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:693:Deliver the core runtime contracts: stop-reason taxonomy, legal-stop gates, resume semantics, audit journal, and hypothesis verification ledger. This sub-phase maps directly to 042 Phase 1 but is adapted for improvement loop semantics (e.g., `promoted` and `rolledBack` replace deep-research `synthesized` states).

I’m also finishing the reducer read, because the optimizer’s real search space depends on what the reducer actually computes today versus what the phase spec assumes it can tune.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2226:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2102:/bin/zsh -lc 'for fam in 028 040 042; do echo "===== FAMILY $fam ====="; find .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration -type f '"\\( -path \"*"'${fam}-*/research/deep-research-state.jsonl" -o -path "*${fam}-*/review/deep-review-state.jsonl" -o -path "*${fam}-*/research/findings-registry.json" -o -path "*${fam}-*/review/deep-review-findings-registry.json" -o -path "*${fam}-*/research/deep-research-dashboard.md" -o -path "*${fam}-*/review/deep-review-dashboard.md" -o -path "*${fam}-*/research/iterations/iteration-*.md" -o -path "*${fam}-*/review/iterations/iteration-*.md" '"\\) | sort; echo; echo 'counts'; printf 'research state jsonl: '; find .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration -type f -path \"*"'${fam}-*/research/deep-research-state.jsonl" | wc -l; printf '"'review state jsonl: '; find .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration -type f -path \"*"'${fam}-*/review/deep-review-state.jsonl" | wc -l; printf '"'research iterations: '; find .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration -type f -path \"*"'${fam}-*/research/iterations/iteration-*.md" | wc -l; printf '"'review iterations: '; find .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration -type f -path \"*"'${fam}-*/review/iterations/iteration-*.md" | wc -l; echo; done' in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2243:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6522:    23	This packet converts the consolidated agentic-systems research into one implementation plan for the two autonomous deep-loop products: `sk-deep-research` and `sk-deep-review`. The work is centered on runtime truth, not feature sprawl: real stop reasons, legal completion gates, resume-from-run semantics, auditability, claim verification, behavior-first tests, and richer dashboards that make loop state easier to trust and resume.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2244:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6524:    25	**Key Decisions**: keep research and review as explicit products instead of collapsing them into a generic workflow DSL; make council synthesis and coordination-board support opt-in instead of default; treat journals, ledgers, and behavior tests as first-class runtime artifacts.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2245:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6552:    53	The current deep-loop stack is functional, but it still treats "converged" as a mostly inferred state instead of a strongly explained runtime truth. Deep research and deep review already have JSONL state, dashboards, and reducer/parity coverage, yet the consolidated research shows clear gaps around stop-reason taxonomy, legal done gates, resume/start-from behavior, research claim verification, audit trails, richer dashboards, behavior-first test coverage, and large-run coordination.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2247:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6571:    72	- Append-only audit-journal planning for both loops.
