/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '150,245p' .opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_improve-agent_confirm.yaml" in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
 succeeded in 0ms:
        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_start --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"target\":\"{target_path}\",\"charter\":\"{spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-charter.md\",\"startedAt\":\"{started_at}\"}'"

  phase_loop:
    description: "Iterative propose -> score -> benchmark -> reduce cycle with operator gates"
    repeat: true
    max_repeats: "{max_iterations}"
    steps:
      step_scan_integration:
        description: "Refresh integration surface map before each iteration"
        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/scan-integration.cjs --agent={target_profile} --output={spec_folder}/improvement/integration-report.json"
      gate_before_candidate:
        type: approval_gate
        description: "Operator authorizes candidate generation for this iteration"
        present: |
          Ready to generate candidate #{iteration} for `{target_path}`.
          Options:
          A) Generate candidate
          B) Stop loop
      step_generate_candidate:
        description: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write one bounded candidate"
        action: "Dispatch @improve-agent to write a new candidate into candidates/"
      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_generated:
        description: "Emit candidate_generated journal event after the candidate is written"
        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_generated --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"candidatePath\":\"{candidate_path}\"}'"
      step_score_candidate:
        description: "Score candidate across 5 dimensions (dynamic) or keyword checks (static)"
        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/score-candidate.cjs --candidate={candidate_path} --baseline={target_path} --manifest={spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc --profile={target_profile} --target={target_path} --output={score_output_path}"
      step_emit_journal_event_candidate_scored:
        description: "Emit candidate_scored journal event after scoring completes"
        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit candidate_scored --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"candidateId\":\"{candidate_id}\",\"scoreOutputPath\":\"{score_output_path}\",\"weightedScore\":\"{weighted_score}\"}'"
      step_run_benchmark:
        description: "Run fixture tests against packet-local outputs"
        action: "Run profile fixtures against packet-local outputs under benchmark-runs/{target_profile}/"
      step_append_ledger:
        description: "Record scored results in append-only JSONL ledger"
        action: "Append prompt-score and benchmark records into agent-improvement-state.jsonl"
      step_reduce:
        description: "Refresh dashboard, registry, and dimensional progress"
        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs {spec_folder}/improvement"
      gate_after_score:
        type: approval_gate
        description: "Operator reviews scores and decides next action"
        present: |
          Candidate scored. Review dimensional progress in dashboard.
          Options:
          A) Continue to next iteration
          B) Promote accepted candidate (handover only, requires evidence)
          C) Stop loop
      step_emit_journal_event_gate_evaluation:
        description: "Emit gate_evaluation journal event after the operator gate is evaluated"
        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit gate_evaluation --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"iteration\":\"{iteration}\",\"gateName\":\"gate_after_score\",\"gateDecision\":\"{gate_after_score}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\"}'"
      step_promote_candidate:
        description: "Promote the accepted candidate to canonical target (guarded, requires score evidence)"
        condition: "gate_after_score == 'B' AND score_recommendation == 'candidate-better'"
        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/promote-candidate.cjs --candidate={candidate_path} --target={target_path} --score={score_output_path} --benchmark-report={benchmark_output_path} --repeatability-report={repeatability_output_path} --config={spec_folder}/improvement/agent-improvement-config.json --manifest={spec_folder}/improvement/target-manifest.jsonc --archive-dir={spec_folder}/improvement/archive --approve"

  phase_synthesis:
    description: "After loop exits, summarize findings and recommend next action"
    steps:
      step_final_dashboard:
        description: "Present final dashboard with per-dimension trends and stop reason"
        action: "Read dashboard.md and present summary to operator"
      step_recommendation:
        description: "Recommend continue, promote, or stop based on reducer output"
        action: "Check experiment-registry.json for bestPromptRecord and stopStatus"
      step_emit_journal_event:
        description: "Emit session_end journal event after synthesis completes"
        command: "node .opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs --emit session_end --journal {spec_folder}/improvement/improvement-journal.jsonl --details '{\"sessionId\":\"{session_id}\",\"stopReason\":\"{stop_reason}\",\"sessionOutcome\":\"{session_outcome}\",\"endedAt\":\"{ended_at}\",\"totalIterations\":\"{total_iterations}\"}'"

codex
**Findings**
1. `F-017-001` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `correctness` | File: `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_improve-agent_auto.yaml` | Line: `168-183`
Title: `sk-improve-agent` never emits the first-class legal-stop events that the other reducers depend on
Description: The improve-agent runtime truth contract says legal-stop evaluation must produce `legal_stop_evaluated` and, on failure, `blocked_stop` with failed gates. The visible auto workflow only emits `gate_evaluation` and `session_end`; the confirm workflow mirrors the same omission at `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_improve-agent_confirm.yaml:198-217`. That leaves `sk-improve-agent` as the outlier versus research/review, where reducer-visible stop-gate events are first-class state. Because those events never exist, the improver reducer cannot replay gate failures, blocked-stop reasons, or blocked-stop history the way the other two reducers can.
Evidence: `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/assets/improvement_charter.md:84-85,104` requires `legal_stop_evaluated` plus `blocked_stop` with full gate results, but the shipped workflow only emits `gate_evaluation` at `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_improve-agent_auto.yaml:168-170` and then jumps to `session_end` at `182-183`.
Recommendation: Add explicit `legal_stop_evaluated` and `blocked_stop` journal emissions to both improve-agent workflows, include the gate bundle and failed-gate list in the emitted payload, and extend the reducer with a `blockedStopHistory`-style consumer/dashboard section so the improver loop matches research/review runtime-truth replay.

2. `F-017-002` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `correctness` | File: `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Line: `835-844, 761-813`
Title: Improve-agent reducer reads replay artifacts into the registry but drops them from the dashboard
Description: The reducer loads `journalSummary`, `candidateLineage`, and `mutationCoverage` into the registry, but `renderDashboard()` never renders any of them. That breaks the shipped Phase 008 contract that operators should see the replay-consumer summaries in the dashboard itself, not only in raw JSON. Research/review dashboards both surface their reducer-derived runtime-truth sections; improve-agent currently hides its equivalent data.
Evidence: The reducer stores the fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:837-839`, but `renderDashboard()` only outputs Global Summary, Sample Quality, Guardrails, Stop Status, per-profile sections, and Recommendation at `761-813`. In the low-sample fixture, the reducer produces non-null `journalSummary`, `candidateLineage`, and `mutationCoverage`, while the rendered dashboard only contains `## Sample Quality`. This also contradicts `.opencode/changelog/15--sk-improve-agent/v1.2.0.0.md:85`, which says the dashboard exposes the “full journal/lineage/coverage summary.”
Recommendation: Add dedicated dashboard sections for journal summary, candidate lineage, and mutation coverage, and add a regression test that asserts those sections appear when the replay artifacts are present and degrade cleanly to `null`/`n/a` when absent.

3. `F-017-003` | Severity: `P1` | Dimension: `correctness` | File: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Line: `217-237`
Title: Research graph convergence scoring collapses to `0` when `blendedScore` is absent
Description: The research reducer only accepts `signals.blendedScore` or `signals.score`. The state-format contract says `blendedScore` is included “when available,” which makes it optional. When a valid `graph_convergence` event carries only the typed signal bundle (`questionCoverage`, `claimVerificationRate`, `contradictionDensity`, etc.), the reducer still records the decision and blockers but forces `graphConvergenceScore` to `0`. That produces a false “no graph support” signal in the registry/dashboard and diverges from the review reducer, which already handles alternate names and numeric fallback.
Evidence: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:317` says `signals` includes `blendedScore` “when available.” With the same event payload `{questionCoverage:0.86, claimVerificationRate:0.78, contradictionDensity:0.04}`, `sk-deep-research` returns `{graphConvergenceScore:0}`, while `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:392-414` computes `0.56` from the numeric signals.
Recommendation: Port the review-side `computeGraphConvergenceScore()` logic into the research reducer or otherwise add a fallback that derives a score when `blendedScore` is absent, then add a parity test covering a `graph_convergence` event without `blendedScore`.

4. `F-017-004` | Severity: `P2` | Dimension: `correctness` | File: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs` | Line: `437-446, 857-861`
Title: Review dashboard’s `ACTIVE RISKS` path bypasses blocker normalization and can regress to `[object Object]`
Description: The reducer added `normalizeBlockedByList()` specifically so legacy structured blocker entries do not render as `[object Object]` in the dashboard. The `BLOCKED STOPS` section uses the normalized history, but `ACTIVE RISKS` bypasses that work and joins `latestBlockedStop.blockedBy` directly from the raw JSONL event. If a legacy packet or graph-blocker payload still carries structured entries, the main risk summary can regress even though the blocked-stop section is fixed.
Evidence: The normalization helper is explicitly documented at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:437-445`, but `ACTIVE RISKS` reads the raw event and does `latestBlockedStop.blockedBy.join(', ')` at `857-861` instead of using `buildBlockedStopHistory()` output.
Recommendation: Reuse the normalized entry from `registry.blockedStopHistory.at(-1)` inside `ACTIVE RISKS`, or call `normalizeBlockedByList()` there before rendering.

**Iteration Summary**
Total findings: `4`
Breakdown: `0 P0`, `3 P1`, `1 P2`

**Coverage Assessment**
Reviewed about `95%` of the requested focus area: all three `reduce-state.cjs` implementations, the improve-agent journal helper and visible YAML emitters, the 042 packet phase docs/changelog claims tied to reducer/runtime-truth behavior, and reducer smoke checks on live packet/fixture data. I did not do a full mirror sweep of `.claude/`, `.codex/`, and `.gemini` runtime mirrors in this iteration.

**Confidence**
`high`

**Next Priorities**
1. Verify the improve-agent end-to-end write path for `legal_stop_evaluated` / `blocked_stop` with a real fixture or workflow run, then add reducer/dashboard assertions for those events.
2. Add parity tests across research/review for `graph_convergence` payloads that omit `blendedScore`.
3. Re-check parser-sensitive reducer inputs against the current agent templates and iteration-output contracts, especially around future `iteration-017` output formatting and partial-write recovery.
